topic: Why Gyms Want You to Never Show Up

packaging.title: Why Gyms Want You to Never Show Up
altTitles: The Real Reason Gyms Oversell Memberships
How Gyms Profit From Empty Treadmills
packaging.description: A deep dive into the gym membership business model, why facilities sell far more memberships than they can hold, and how no-shows quietly fund the entire industry.

thumbnail.visualIdea: an empty gym floor plan with rows of treadmills and a single lonely figure

packaging.hookPromise: Your gym sold roughly ten times more memberships than it could ever physically hold — and its entire profit model depends on you breaking your New Year's resolution.
openLoops: How many members can a gym actually fit at once?
Why is the cancellation process designed to be a maze?
Why does January pricing target people who won't last?
packaging.payoff: A typical gym holds a few hundred people but sells thousands of memberships; the business is engineered around the ~50% who stop coming by month two, whose dues are pure profit.
packaging.whyItWorks: Almost everyone has paid for a gym they stopped using; the oversell number turns personal guilt into an exposed industry trick.
